function.name: database_list_info
function.description: List all user-approved SQLite databases available to Strappy with safe basic file metadata, a short human description, and availability state without full filesystem paths. Use database_context_read with a returned database_id for schema and remembered database context.

function.name: database_query
function.description: Run one bounded read-only SQL query against a user-approved SQLite database. The model supplies only the assistant-visible database_id and SQL query; Strappy resolves the local path, opens the database read-only, and enforces statement, row, and result limits.

sql.description: One read-only SQL SELECT or EXPLAIN query. Do not include PRAGMA, ATTACH, writes, multiple statements, filesystem paths, open flags, or limit settings. Large 64-bit integer IDs may be returned as decimal strings to preserve exact values. For typeless columns, sample typeof(column) and values before filtering because flag values may be text; if a numeric-looking flag comparison returns zero rows after a count showed rows exist, retry with quoted text or CAST(column AS TEXT). If a BLOB column appears to store text, select substr(CAST(column AS TEXT),1,N) instead of selecting the raw BLOB.

function.name: helper_convert_dates
function.description: Convert a comma-separated list of numeric timestamps to a comma-separated list of UTC ISO8601 timestamps. The timestamps argument is required; the optional unit selects unix_seconds, unix_milliseconds, unix_microseconds, unix_nanoseconds, apple_seconds, apple_milliseconds, apple_microseconds, or apple_nanoseconds. The default unit is unix_seconds.

timestamps.description: Comma-separated numeric timestamps to convert. Preserve the order; do not include labels or surrounding brackets.

unit.description: Timestamp unit and epoch. Defaults to unix_seconds when omitted.

function.name: helper_user_info_read
function.description: Read remembered stable user facts such as names, relationships, and preferences. Use this when a user request depends on personal context that may have been mentioned in a previous session.

query.description: Optional text search across subject, predicate, and value.

kind.description: Optional exact kind filter such as identity, relationship, or preference.

limit.description: Optional maximum facts to return. Defaults to 20; maximum is 50.

function.name: helper_user_info_remember
function.description: Remember one small stable user fact as structured data. Store only useful personal facts that are explicit or strongly implied in the conversation; do not store secrets, credentials, or long copied content.

kind.description: Fact category, such as identity, relationship, preference, or project.

subject.description: Who or what the fact is about, for example user, Steve, or email_summary.

predicate.description: Relationship or property name, for example first_name or is_user_parent.

value.description: Small stable fact value. Do not store secrets, credentials, or long copied content.

confidence.description: Optional confidence from 0.0 to 1.0. Defaults to 0.75.

source.description: Optional short source label such as user_explicit or model_observed.

function.name: helper_user_info_forget
function.description: Forget one remembered user fact by exact id returned from helper_user_info_read.

id.description: Exact fact id returned by helper_user_info_read.

function.name: database_context_read
function.description: Read database context. Requires at least one of database_id, query, kind, or limit. With a database_id, returns safe metadata, a human description, simplified live schema, and remembered database hints such as join paths, column semantics, content locations, and caveats. Without a database_id, use query, kind, or limit to read remembered database hints only.

database_id.description: Optional assistant-visible database_id returned by database_list_info. When provided, the result includes metadata, description, and simplified live schema for that selected database.

query.description: Optional text search across title, content, and evidence.

kind.description: Optional exact kind filter such as join_hint, column_semantics, query_pattern, caveat, or content_location.

limit.description: Optional maximum hints to return. Defaults to 20; maximum is 50.

function.name: helper_database_info_remember
function.description: Remember one small database hint for an approved database. Store scoped, evidence-backed facts such as join recipes or content extraction patterns, not arbitrary database content.

kind.description: Hint category, such as join_hint, column_semantics, query_pattern, caveat, or content_location.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the database hint.

content.description: Scoped database hint. Store query recipes or schema observations, not arbitrary row content.

evidence.description: Optional short evidence, sample query, or sampled-result note.

confidence.description: Optional confidence from 0.0 to 1.0. Defaults to 0.75.

function.name: helper_database_info_forget
function.description: Forget one remembered database hint by exact id returned from database_context_read.

id.description: Exact database hint id returned by database_context_read.
